en.extract:
The Tibetan bunting (Emberiza koslowi) is a species of bird in the family Emberizidae. It is endemic to eastern side of the Tibetan Plateau.


Etymology
The specific name "koslowi" for this species was given after Russian explorer Pyotr Kozlov.


Description
The crown is black and there are white stripes at the head. The back is chestnut coloured.


Behaviour
The domed nest structure of this species appears to be unique amongst the Emberizinae buntings which have open nest structures. Female lays 3 or 4 eggs.
They eat grains in winter and insects, like butterflies, grasshoppers and beetles, in summer.
Main predators of Tibetan bunting are birds of prey like falcons and owls and mammals like foxes, weasels and badgers.


References


External links
Tibetan bunting photos and audio at The Macaulay Library of Cornell Lab of Ornithology

The '''Tibetan bunting''' ('''''Emberiza koslowi''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Emberizidae]]. It is [[endemic]] to eastern side of the [[Tibetan Plateau]].<ref name="iucn status" />

==Etymology==
The specific name "''koslowi''" for this species was given after Russian explorer [[Pyotr Kozlov]].<ref>{{cite book|last=Beolens|first=Bo|title=The Eponym Dictionary of Mammals|url=https://archive.org/details/eponymdictionary00beol|url-access=limited|year=2009|publisher=The Johns Hopkins University Press|isbn=978-0-8018-9304-9|author2=Michael Watkins |author3=Mike Grayson |page=[https://archive.org/details/eponymdictionary00beol/page/n245 229]}}</ref>

==Description==
The crown is black and there are white stripes at the head. The back is chestnut coloured.<ref name=SmithMag>{{cite web|last=McKenna|first=Phil|title=A Buddhist Monk Saves One of the World's Rarest Birds|url=http://www.smithsonianmag.com/science-nature/A-Buddhist-Monk-Saves-One-of-the-Worlds-Rarest-Birds.html#|work=Smithsonian Magazine|publisher=Smithsonian Institution|access-date=31 December 2012|date=October 2011}}</ref>

==Behaviour==
The domed nest structure of this species appears to be unique amongst the [[Emberizinae]] buntings which have open nest structures.<ref>{{cite journal|last=Thewlis|first=R.M.|author2=R.P. Martins|title=Observations of the breeding biology and behaviour of Kozlov's Bunting Emberiza koslowi|journal=Forktail|year=2000|volume=16|pages=57–59|url=http://www.orientalbirdclub.org/publications/forktail/16pdfs/Thewlis-Kozlovs.pdf|access-date=31 December 2012|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110610210725/http://www.orientalbirdclub.org/publications/forktail/16pdfs/Thewlis-Kozlovs.pdf|archive-date=10 June 2011}}</ref> Female lays 3 or 4 eggs.<ref name=SmithMag/>

They eat grains in winter and [[insect]]s, like [[butterfly|butterflies]], [[grasshopper]]s and [[beetle]]s, in summer.<ref name=SmithMag/>

Main predators of Tibetan bunting are [[Bird of prey|birds of prey]] like [[falcon]]s and [[owl]]s and [[mammals]] like [[fox]]es, [[weasel]]s and [[badger]]s.<ref name=SmithMag/>

zh.extract:
藏鹀（学名：Emberiza koslowi）为雀科鹀属的鸟类，是中国的特有物种。分布于青海、昌都北部澜沧江上游一带等地，主要生活于高山草甸、草原和灌丛鸟类以及栖于青藏高原山柳灌丛地带。该物种的模式产地在青海南部和澜沧江上游的杂楚河。
